shell_best_practices.overview: Best practices for the devops-concert-shell mode when executed by Bob Shell inside GitHub Actions. These guidelines emphasize deterministic automation, GitHub MCP first, and clean workflow phase boundaries.

principle.name: Single-phase completion
principle.description: Complete the entire package remediation phase in one shell run. Do not pause inside Bob waiting for human feedback or approval.

principle.name: MCP-first GitHub integration
principle.description: Use GitHub MCP for supported GitHub operations before considering fallback tools. Fallback is acceptable only when MCP fails or cannot provide the required data or action.

principle.name: Deterministic output
principle.description: Final output must contain stable, parseable fields so GitHub Actions can extract PR metadata reliably.

principle.name: Manifest-only scope
principle.description: Restrict changes to dependency manifests, lockfiles, and package metadata relevant to the CVE remediation.

in_scope.item: Read the triggering issue and extract package/CVE context
Validate the recommendation with public advisory sources
Update the package manifest and related dependency metadata
Commit, push, and create a PR

out_of_scope.item: Running unit tests as a required completion gate
Waiting for approval inside Bob Shell

mode_boundaries.boundary_rule: If package upgrades later trigger test failures or compatibility issues, this mode still completes its package remediation phase and leaves subsequent phases to the downstream workflow.

practice.rule: Create or switch to the remediation branch before editing files
Keep commits tightly scoped to the vulnerability remediation
Push the exact remediation branch that will be used as the PR head
Extract and report the final PR URL and number from the GitHub operation response

preferred_actions.item: Fetch issue details with GitHub MCP
Create remote branches with GitHub MCP when available
Create the pull request with GitHub MCP
Add PR comments, labels, and reviewers with GitHub MCP when supported

fallback_guidance.item: Use local git for branch checkout, commit, and push
Use `gh` or GitHub REST API only after an MCP failure or unsupported action
Document the fallback step and reason in the final output if fallback was needed

quality_checklist.item: Issue context was resolved from the prompt or repository metadata without interactive questioning
GitHub MCP was attempted first for supported GitHub tasks
Concert recommendation was captured and validated externally
Only manifest or dependency metadata files were changed
Commit message references the CVE and issue number
Final output includes PR_NUMBER, PR_URL, and PR_BRANCH

anti_pattern.description: Using broad repository issue listing when the workflow already provides a specific issue
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Fetch the exact triggering issue directly

anti_pattern.description: Switching to `gh` immediately without trying GitHub MCP
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Attempt MCP first and fallback only on failure

anti_pattern.description: Performing application-code remediation in the DevOps shell phase
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Keep to manifest-only scope and let downstream workflow phases handle later concerns
